rationale: Both correctly land on the ground-truth verdict (duplicate of #4808), but X states as settled fact that #4808 is merged and shows the exact post-fix line/comment, plus invents suspiciously specific artifacts (a test_kv_cache_scale_mapper.py file, unrelated 'qwen2_old.py'/'mammoth_moda2.py' loaders) with no hedge — risky given the reporter hit this crash on a recent main commit, suggesting the fix may not actually be live. Y reaches the same duplicate conclusion but explicitly flags that #4808 shows closed/unmerged and that the regression test has a coverage gap at this exact file, honestly noting what it could vs couldn't verify — better calibrated grounding and more useful to a maintainer even though it's more hedged.
